title: serruria confragosa
intro: Serruria confragosa, also known as the Cape sundew, is a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae that is native to the southwestern region of Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Serruria confragosa are said to include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Serruria confragosa can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make a topical cream or ointment for skin conditions.
